advancement.simulated.my_eye.desc: Shine a laser into a Laser Sensor and activate it
advancement.simulated.nearsighted.desc: Obtain and place an Optical Sensor to show you what's right there
advancement.simulated.no_pressure.desc: Obtain and place an Altitude Sensor
advancement.simulated.not_gonna_sugarcoa: Use Honey Glue to connect a group of blocks for assembly
advancement.simulated.opposite_attract.d: Place and power a Redstone Magnet
advancement.simulated.rewind_time.desc: Watch a Torsion Spring unwind to its original position
advancement.simulated.speed_is_key.desc: Obtain and place a Velocity Sensor to satiate your need for speed
advancement.simulated.steamless_engine.d: Place and power a Portable Engine
advancement.simulated.that_should_do_for:
Place over 10 hours of fuel into a Portable Engine

advancement.simulated.thataway.desc: Obtain and place a Navigation Table to point you in the right direction
advancement.simulated.the_definition_of_: Obtain and place a Gimbal Sensor to help you keep balance
advancement.simulated.unpowered_steering: Grab and spin a Steering Wheel
advancement.simulated.you_spin_me_right_: Assemble a Simulated Contraption using a Swivel Bearing
block.simulated.handle.tooltip.behaviour: _Grabs_ the handle, moving yourself around it.
_Grabs_ the handle, moving only the _Simulated Contraption_ the handle is on.
Adjusts the _distance_, moving the handle closer or further away
block.simulated.handle.tooltip.summary: Attach to a block to easily _grab_ onto it.
block.simulated.linked_typewriter.toolti: _Toggles_ the Linked Typewriter. _Movement controls_ and any _assigned keys_ are taken over while it's active.
Binds the _Linked Controller's_ frequencies to the typewriter.
Enables _Bind Mode_, press _any key_ to bind it to the _Link's Frequency_. (Frequencies are _saved_ when the Linked Typewriter is _broken_)
R-Click Item on Redstone Link Receiver
Grants convenient control over many _Redstone Link_ frequencies assigned to its _keys_.
block.simulated.nameplate.tooltip.behavi: _Changes the color_ of the name on the Nameplate
_Locks_ the name displayed on the Nameplate
All Nameplates will _share and set_ the name of the _Simulated Contraption_
block.simulated.nameplate.tooltip.condit: When used on a Simulated Contraption
generator.simulated.airship_ready.info: A Superflat world at y=63 to simulate normal air pressure conditions.
item.simulated.contraption_diagram.toolt: A _visual diagram_ with detailed information about a _Simulated Contraption_.
item.simulated.creative_physics_staff.to: The _Targeted Contraption_ will become _locked in place_.
_Toggle dragging_ the _Targeted Contraption_ around with the staff.
Rotate the _Targeted Contraption_ with the staff.
Adjusts _how far away_ you are _dragging_ the _Targeted Contraption_
_Fires_ a rope-attached _Plunger_. Two fired Plungers will _connect_ and _pull attached blocks together_.
_No_ _Durability_ will be used. Instead, _Air_ _pressure_ is drained from the Tank
Slomhp! Move _Simulated Contraptions_ with the power of suction. Can be powered with _Air_ _Pressure_ from a _Backtank_.
simulated.analog_transmission.too_fast_e: It appears that this _Analog Transmission_ is attempting to exceed the _maximum rotation speed_. Reduce or remove its _Redstone Signal_ to slow it down.
simulated.contraption_diagram.cannot_use: This Diagram must be on a Simulated Contraption to be inspected...
simulated.gui.altitude_sensor.title: Configure Signal Range
simulated.gui.assembly.exception.couldNo: Could not align Simulated Contraption to disassemble!
simulated.gui.assembly.exception.outOfWo: Cannot disassemble out of the world!
simulated.gui.assembly.exception.tooFarF: Too far from the ground to disassemble!
simulated.linked_typewriter.bind_item: Press any key to bind this frequency to the respective key
simulated.linked_typewriter.confirm_dele: This will delete all current keybinds. Click again to confirm.
simulated.linked_typewriter.press_keybin: Press any key to bind this Remote's frequencies to the Linked Typewriter
simulated.linked_typewriter.title: Linked Typewriter
simulated.merging_glue.not_enough_suppor: This face does not have enough support!
simulated.merging_glue.only_between_sub_: Can only glue between Simulated Contraptions!
simulated.merging_glue.same_sub_level: Cannot glue on the same Simulated Contraption!
simulated.ponder.altitude_sensor_into.te: The Altitude Sensor outputs a redstone signal based on its elevation
By default, travelling upwards increases the signal strength
The minimum and maximum altitudes can be changed in the UI
It can also be set to decrease in strength as it travels upwards
When wearing Engineers' Goggles, information about air pressure and exact altitude can be seen
A Wrench can also be used to see more precise changes in elevation
simulated.ponder.analog_transmission.hea: Controlling rotational speed using Analog Transmission
simulated.ponder.analog_transmission.tex: Unpowered Analog Transmissions behave exactly like Encased Cogwheels
Analog redstone inputs slows down the cogwheel relative to the shaft
Higher signal strengths results in larger gear ratios
When the Transmission gets its input through the cogwheel, the shaft speeds up instead
At full signal input or breaking speeds, the shaft and cogwheel disconnect...
...and can then rotate independently of each other
Extracting from Harvesting actors using the Auger Shaft
Auger Shafts collect materials from Harvesting actors
All horizontally connected actors are included
When a connected actor breaks a block...
simulated.ponder.auger_shaft_intro.text_: Powered Auger Shafts will transport items and extract them from actors
Using a Wrench, you can cycle between the Auger Shaft and Auger Cog
Using Industrial Iron Blocks, Auger Shafts can be encased
Item flow can be observed via the indicator on the Auger Cog...
...Or by inspecting the Auger while wearing Goggles
Items cannot be inserted or extracted from the Auger Cog...
simulated.ponder.directional_gearshift.h: Controlling rotational direction using Directional Gearshift
simulated.ponder.directional_gearshift.t: Unpowered Directional Gearshifts do not relay rotational power
When the straight side is powered, it will relay rotation
When the reverse side is powered, it will reverse rotation
When both sides are powered, it will stop relaying rotation
simulated.ponder.directional_receiver.te: Directional Linked Receivers can detect the angle to other nearby Redstone Links
Redstone Links directly infront will be received at their full signal strength
A shallower angle results in a lower received signal strength
Directional Linked Receivers cannot receive signals from Redstone Links behind them
Directional Linked Receivers respect the same item frequencies that Redstone Links use
simulated.ponder.docking_connector.text_: Docking Connectors can create rigid connections in pairs
Extended Connectors attempt to align with each other
Once sufficiently aligned, the Connectors will lock together
The Connection is rigid, but keeps the two sides independent
Once locked, Items and Fluids can be transferred in either direction
Insertion and Extraction must occur on both sides of the connection
Either Connector can be retracted to undock
Redstone Comparators can be used to read the Docking progress
simulated.ponder.gimbal_sensor_intro.tex: The Gimbal Sensor outputs a redstone signal based on its orientation
By default, the signal is emitted downhill
The value boxes can configure the angle of maximum signal...
Each axis operates independently of one another
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_intro.text_1: Honey Glue is a convenient alternative to Super Glue for Simulated Contraptions
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_intro.text_2: Clicking two endpoints creates a new 'glued' area...
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_intro.text_3: ...which can be expanded or retracted via Ctrl-Scrolling
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_intro.text_4: Alternatively, hold ALT to place an endpoint midair
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_intro.text_6: ...Honey Glue does not attach to animated Contraptions
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_intro.text_7: Instead, it exclusively attaches to Simulated Contraptions
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_super_glue.h: Using Honey Glue with Super Glue
simulated.ponder.honey_glue_super_glue.t: Honey Glue may be preferable in this scenario
When assembled, Honey Glue will attach to overlapping Super Glue
However, Super Glue will not attach to overlapping Honey Glue
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.header: Using Laser Pointers and Laser Sensors
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.text_1: Laser pointers emit a laser when powered
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.text_2: The laser can be obstructed by blocks
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.text_3: Laser Sensors can detect the laser when unobstructed
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.text_4: They will emit a signal dependent on the laser power
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.text_6: ...only lasers with matching colors will activate the sensor
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.text_7: Right clicking the front or back face with a wrench inverts redstone power
simulated.ponder.laser_pointer.text_8: The max casting distance can be adjusted on the back
simulated.ponder.modulating_receiver.tex: Modulating Linked Receivers can detect the distance to other nearby Redstone Links
Right-click it to open the Configuration UI
This allows setting a minimum range...
Redstone Links within the minimum range will be received at their full signal strength
Redstone Links outside the maximum range will not be received at all
Redstone Links in-between the two ranges will be received with proportional signal strengths
Modulating Linked Receivers respect the same item frequencies that Redstone Links use
simulated.ponder.navigation_table.text_1: The Navigation Table provides redstone feedback for Navigation Items
simulated.ponder.navigation_table.text_2: When a Navigation Item is provided...
simulated.ponder.navigation_table.text_3: ...it will emit a Redstone signal towards the destination
simulated.ponder.navigation_table.text_4: The Redstone signal is weaker when not directly facing the destination
simulated.ponder.navigation_table.text_5: Different Navigation Items provide unique targets
simulated.ponder.nozzle.text_1: Nozzles attached to fans on Simulated Contraptions push away from nearby surfaces
simulated.ponder.nozzle.text_2: Nearby walls will be pushed away from horizontally
simulated.ponder.nozzle.text_3: Fluids also act as a surface
simulated.ponder.optical_sensor.text_1: Optical Sensors can detect blocks which obstruct its laser pointer
simulated.ponder.optical_sensor.text_2: When a block is detected, a Redstone signal is emitted
simulated.ponder.optical_sensor.text_3: The Optical Sensor's signal strength is relative to its detected block's distance
simulated.ponder.optical_sensor.text_4: The maximum distance can be configured using the value panel
simulated.ponder.optical_sensor.text_6: ...only matching blocks will emit a signal
simulated.ponder.physics_assembler_block: Some blocks may have different weights...
simulated.ponder.physics_assembler_intro: The Physics Assembler assembles blocks into Simulated Contraptions
Use Super Glue or Honey Glue to select a group of blocks...
...then hold right-click and pull the lever to assemble
Note that blocks along diagonal edges are included
Once assembled, the original Physics Assembler is no longer needed...
...and any Physics Assembler can be used for disassembly
simulated.ponder.physics_assembler_simul: Simulated Contraptions have physics, and remain completely interactable when assembled
simulated.ponder.physics_assembler_sub_l: Disconnected sections of Simulated Contraptions will split off
A Slime Ball can be used to merge any two Simulated Contraptions
simulated.ponder.portable_engine.header: Generating Rotational Force using the Portable Engine
simulated.ponder.portable_engine.text_1: The Portable Engine generates rotational force by burning fuel
simulated.ponder.portable_engine.text_2: It can be configured to run in either direction.
simulated.ponder.portable_engine.text_3: Fuel can be inserted by automated means
simulated.ponder.portable_engine.text_4: Using a Blaze Cake, the Portable Engine can be superheated for additional power output
simulated.ponder.redstone_accumulator.te: Redstone Accumulators store an analog signal that can be modified over time
Signals at the back increase the output strength
Signals from the sides decrease the output strength
Using the value panel, the rate of change can be configured
Configured delays can range up to an hour
simulated.ponder.redstone_inductor.text_: Redstone Inductors output an analog signal that changes over time to match its input
Using the value panel, the rate of change can be configured
Configured delays can range up to an hour
simulated.ponder.redstone_magnet.text_1: Redstone Magnets can attract or repel other Redstone Magnets
simulated.ponder.redstone_magnet.text_4: Attracting magnets attempt to align with each other
simulated.ponder.rope_connections.text_1: A pair of Rope Connectors can be attached with Rope
simulated.ponder.rope_connections.text_2: A pair of Rope Winches cannot connect
simulated.ponder.rope_intro.text_1: Right-Clicking a Rope Winch and Connector with a Rope Coupling will attach them together
simulated.ponder.rope_intro.text_2: When given Rotational Force, Rope Winches reel in and out the attached rope
simulated.ponder.rope_intro.text_3: Shears can be used to remove the Rope
simulated.ponder.rope_intro.text_4: Right-Click holding a wrench to slide down the Rope
simulated.ponder.shared.property_tooltip: Detailed property information can be previewed in the item tooltip
Detailed property information can be previewed in the item tooltip while wearing Goggles
However, you can't preview these properties because you disabled the tooltip...
Detailed property information can be previewed in the item tooltip while pressing Shift
Detailed property information can be previewed in the item tooltip while wearing Goggles and pressing Shift
simulated.ponder.steering_wheel_comparat: Redstone Comparators can be used to read the current angle of the Steering Wheel
The output Signal is proportional to the configured maximum Angle
Redstone Comparators placed on the back of the Steering Wheel will read if it is in use
simulated.ponder.steering_wheel_intro.te: Steering Wheels provide precise rotational output
Hold right click to grab the wheel...
Sneaking while turning the wheel snaps to 45° intervals
The maximum turning angle can be configured using the value panel
Mind that Bearings have to be specifically told not to disassemble
Use wood planks on the wheel to change its appearance
simulated.ponder.swivel_bearing_intro.he: Moving Structures using the Swivel Bearing
simulated.ponder.swivel_bearing_intro.te: Swivel Bearings attach to the block in front of them
Use Super Glue or Honey Glue to select a group of blocks
When powered via the cog, it will assemble into a Simulated Contraption
simulated.ponder.swivel_bearing_passthro: Passing Rotation through a Swivel Bearing
Rotational power via the Shaft passes directly through the Swivel Bearing
Rotation remains uninterrupted as the Swivel Bearing turns
simulated.ponder.swivel_bearing_unlockin: This behavior can be configured using the value panel
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail.header: Using Sails and Symmetric Sails on Simulated Contraptions
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail.text_11: Unlike Regular Sails, Symmetric Sails only produce Drag
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail.text_12: Note that when moving parallel to the direction of motion, they have no effect
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail.text_13: When angled away from the direction of motion, Drag is applied
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail.text_14: This can be used for turning or stabilization surfaces
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail.text_3: When moving on a Simulated Contraption, Regular Sails provide Lift
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail.text_4: Lift is generated relative to the speed of the Sail, and is applied perpendicular to its surface
simulated.ponder.symmetric_sail_windmill: Symmetric Sails are handy blocks to create Windmills with
They will attach to blocks and each other without the need of Super Glue or Chassis Blocks
Right-Click with Dye to paint them
simulated.ponder.tag.navigation_items.de: Components which offer a destination to a Navigation Table
simulated.ponder.tag.physics_behavior.de: Components which have unique physics behavior or interactions
simulated.ponder.tag.physics_sensors.des: Components which provide dynamic information about the world around them
simulated.ponder.tag.thrust_blocks.descr: Components which produce thrust on Simulated Contraptions
simulated.ponder.throttle_lever.header: Controlling signals using the Throttle Lever
simulated.ponder.throttle_lever.text_1: Throttle Levers make for a compact and precise source of redstone power
simulated.ponder.throttle_lever.text_2: Right-click and drag to set its analog power output
simulated.ponder.throttle_lever.text_3: Using a Wrench, the signal can be inverted
simulated.ponder.torsion_spring.header: Controlling Rotational Speed using Torsion Springs
simulated.ponder.torsion_spring.text_1: Torsion Springs relay rotation within a set range of angles
simulated.ponder.torsion_spring.text_2: When the input stops rotating, the Spring returns to its starting angle
simulated.ponder.torsion_spring.text_3: The angle limit can be configured on the value panel
simulated.ponder.torsion_spring.text_4: Mind that Bearings have to be specifically told not to disassemble
simulated.ponder.torsion_spring.text_5: When powered by Redstone, Torsion Springs will not spring back
simulated.ponder.torsion_spring.text_6: Redstone Comparators can be used to read the current angle of the spring
simulated.ponder.velocity_sensor.text_1: The Velocity Sensor outputs a redstone signal based on its speed
simulated.ponder.velocity_sensor.text_2: By default, power is output away from the direction of motion
simulated.ponder.velocity_sensor.text_3: The value box can configure the speed of maximum signal
simulated.ponder.velocity_sensor.text_4: It can also change the output side to be towards the direction of motion
simulated.ponder.velocity_sensor.text_5: Velocity is only measured through the facing axis
simulated.prevent_disassembly: Disassembly from non-primary Physics Assembler prevented
simulated.spring.not_enough_support: This face does not have enough support!
simulated.swivel_bearing.too_fast_error: It appears that this _Swivel Bearing_ is rotating _too fast_.
